briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 31, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis Warning and Deepening Impacts
sections.content: A climate report released today warned that greenhouse gas emissions over the past 30 years have caused massive economic damage totaling 51 trillion dollars globally, emphasizing that climate change is no longer a future threat but an unavoidable reality [7, 177, 179]. In particular, direct damages from extreme weather events were reported, including 45 deaths due to five consecutive days of heavy rain in Afghanistan and Pakistan. Analysis also suggests that climate change and the El Niño phenomenon are pushing the Earth beyond its limits [160, 176]. Furthermore, increased pollen counts and the prolonged and exacerbated allergy seasons due to climate change are emerging as new threats to public health [163, 169]. Complex impacts across ecosystems and the economy are also being observed, such as declining salmon populations in Japan and a chocolate price crisis [170, 174]. Amidst these circumstances, political hurdles persist, with some regions conducting related research without using the term 'climate change' [6, 12, 164], underscoring the urgent need for broader awareness of the severity of the climate crisis.

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality Practices and Ecosystem Restoration
sections.content: Various efforts toward a carbon-neutral society are continuing both domestically and internationally. A research team at Kookmin University has embarked on ecosystem restoration research to enhance climate change adaptation and carbon sinks, emphasizing the importance of nature-based solutions [1]. Several institutions, including Korea National University of Agriculture and Fisheries, are promoting carbon neutrality practices through tree-planting events commemorating Arbor Day [26, 37, 38, 67]. Numerous local governments, such as Yeosu City, Yangpyeong County, and Haenam County, are implementing concrete plans and actions for climate change response and carbon neutrality, including preparing for the UNFCCC Climate Week, organizing customized education, and establishing AI-based future agriculture platforms [2, 8, 11, 14, 15, 20]. Notably, Osan City and Gyeonggi Province have introduced a 'Climate Action Opportunity Income' program, offering rewards of up to 20,000 to 90,000 KRW for citizen-participatory carbon neutrality practices [27, 34, 42, 55, 60, 61], thereby encouraging active citizen engagement. Concurrently, movements toward carbon neutrality are spreading across diverse sectors, including special lectures on carbon neutrality in construction materials, carbon neutrality challenges in nursing facilities, and international reports on beavers accidentally contributing to climate change solutions [33, 73, 173].

sections.title: ⚡️ Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Energy Security
sections.content: As instability in the Middle East escalates, the importance of energy security is being highlighted globally, further accelerating the grand transition to renewable energy [84, 85, 107, 109, 130]. President Lee has defined the transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy as an unpostponable national task and ordered relevant ministries to expedite efforts [106, 111, 114, 116, 139]. The Ministry of Climate has launched full-scale support, including allocating a supplementary budget of 524.5 billion KRW for renewable energy financial support and expanded deployment [88, 91, 97, 99, 119, 120, 125, 141, 144, 151]. Notably, plans are being considered to expand electric vehicle purchase support in regions with high renewable energy production [101, 112, 121, 124, 134, 147]. Significant progress has also been made in the Jeju region, where new renewable energy generation permits have resumed following the de-designation of grid management substations [87, 105, 115, 152]. While the European Union (EU) is also accelerating its renewable energy transition by setting a target of 90% greenhouse gas reduction by 2040 [129], domestic companies are leading the market through the expansion of renewable energy businesses and technological innovation [70, 110, 113].

sections.title: 🌱 Strengthening Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience
sections.content: Practical adaptation efforts to minimize the unavoidable impacts of climate change and enhance societal resilience are gaining attention. A research team at Kookmin University has embarked on ecosystem restoration research for climate change adaptation, exploring the potential of nature-based solutions [1]. Yangpyeong County and Donggwangyang Nonghyup are strengthening their climate change response capabilities through customized education and exploration of advanced agricultural practices abroad, respectively [8, 9]. Notably, Haenam County is developing smart adaptation strategies in the agricultural sector, including establishing an AI-based future agriculture innovation platform to enhance climate change response research and promoting the distribution of micro-sprinkler systems for stable fruit production [11, 16, 20]. In terms of social safety nets, Jeju Province has introduced the nation's first 'Construction Site Climate Insurance' for daily construction workers exposed to heatwaves [23, 159], providing proactive measures to compensate for workers' income loss even in extreme weather conditions. Furthermore, examples of climate change adaptation, such as advanced snowmaking technology at a New Hampshire ski resort [162] and Jeju Island's plan to convert rental cars to electric vehicles and introduce an 'electricity forecasting system' [161], demonstrate how various sectors are evolving in response to climate change.

sections.title: 🌍 International Cooperation and Policy Discourse
sections.content: International cooperation and policy discussions for climate change response are actively underway. Yeosu City is earnestly preparing for the 3rd UNFCCC Climate Week event, accelerating preparations to successfully host a large-scale international event with government representatives from 198 signatory nations participating [2, 14, 15, 19, 21]. The Gyeongnam Carbon Neutrality Support Center held a seminar to address the European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) [32], helping domestic industries understand and prepare for changes in the international trade environment. Meanwhile, news that the Director of the Asian Development Bank (ADB) held talks with the Minister of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change [178] raises expectations for increased support and cooperation in climate change response for developing countries. Furthermore, discussions on socio-cultural approaches continue, including the recognition that gender equality is essential for climate change response [166], artworks visualizing climate change [171], and the discourse that climate entertainment can foster dialogue on climate change [167], thereby expanding the scope of climate action.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights that despite the severity of the climate crisis, carbon neutrality practices, renewable energy transition, and diverse adaptation efforts are actively underway both domestically and internationally. We hope that these efforts will continue to expand, paving the way for a climate-resilient future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
